title: salix × grenieri
intro: Salix × grenieri, also known as the hybrid willow, is a cross between the Salix alba and Salix fragilis, and is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The bark of Salix × grenieri contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which makes it useful for medicinal purposes. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Salix × grenieri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
